title: buchnera linearis var. asperata
intro: Buchnera linearis var. asperata, also known as the rough-stemmed Buchnera or the rough-stemmed Buchnera linearis, is a plant species that belongs to the Orobanchaceae family, commonly known as the broomrapes. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating snakebites, and is also known for its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Buchnera linearis var. asperata include its ability to reduce inflammation and fever, as well as its use in treating skin conditions and wounds. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a powder or a decoction, and can also be made into an ointment or a salve for topical application.
